title: The Top 10 Herbal Creams to Combat Anxiety

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool in HUMULUS LUPULUS has been found to be beneficial for anxiety due to its ability to act as a natural anxiolytic by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels by interacting with the brain's GABA receptors.
Linalyl acetate is good for anxiety because it has a calming effect on the body by reducing stress hormones and promoting relaxation, making it a natural remedy for people who suffer from anxiety.

Caryophyllene oxide is good for anxiety because it acts as a natural anxiolytic by binding to brain receptors that regulate stress and emotions, thereby promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of fear and panic.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia cream, also known as lavender oil, is a popular remedy for anxiety. The main reason it works is because of its calming effect on the mind and body. When you apply the cream to your skin, the lavender oil is absorbed into your body, where it interacts with your brain's chemistry. Lavender oil contains compounds that reduce the levels of stress hormones in the body, like cortisol. This decrease in cortisol levels helps to calm your nervous system, making you feel more relaxed and less anxious. Additionally, lavender oil can help improve your sleep quality, which is often disrupted by anxiety. By promoting better sleep, you can wake up feeling refreshed and more able to handle stressful situations. Furthermore, lavender oil has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety symptoms, such as rapid heartbeat and trembling.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Humulus lupulus herbal creams that help with anxiety, start by gathering the necessary ingredients, including dried Humulus lupulus flowers, coconut oil, beeswax, shea butter, and essential oils like lavender or chamomile. First, steep the dried flowers in a carrier oil like coconut oil for 2-3 weeks, shaking the mixture every few days, to create a herbal infusion. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, then add the herbal infusion to the melted mixture and stir well. Remove the bowl from the heat and let the mixture cool and thicken, then stir in some essential oils for their calming properties. Once the mixture has cooled and solidified, transfer it to a jar or tin and label it. To use the cream, simply apply a small amount to the skin, such as the wrists or temples, and gently massage it in. The Humulus lupulus in the cream may help to calm and soothe the mind and body, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for anxiety

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for anxiety because it acts as a natural GABA receptor agonist, helping to slow down nerve activity and calm the brain, thereby reducing feelings of stress and anxiety.
Valeranone is good for anxiety because it helps to calm the mind and body by acting on the brain's GABA receptors, reducing stress and promoting relaxation.
Isovaleric acid is good for anxiety because it has a calming effect on the brain, reducing stress and promoting relaxation by influencing the neurotransmitters responsible for mood regulation.
Valerenal is good for anxiety because it has a calming effect on the brain, helping to slow down rapid thoughts and reduce feelings of stress and nervousness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis cream is made from the root of the valerian plant, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to help people relax and reduce anxiety. The cream is applied directly to the skin, where it is absorbed and helps to calm the nervous system. Valerian root contains chemicals called valerenic acids, which work to slow down brain activity and promote relaxation. This can help to reduce feelings of anxiety and stress, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. By reducing anxiety, valerian cream can also help to improve mood and reduce symptoms of depression. When applied to the skin, the cream can also help to reduce muscle tension and promote a sense of calm, making it a popular remedy for people who experience anxiety and stress.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Humulus lupulus herbal cream that helps with anxiety, you will need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, start by gathering dried Humulus lupulus flowers, also known as hop flowers, which are rich in flavonoids and alpha acids that have a calming effect on the mind and body. You will also need a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, to help the herb infuse into the cream. Next, combine 1/4 cup of dried hop flowers with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar and let it steep in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the herb infuse into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Then, mix the infused oil with a cream base, such as beeswax and coconut oil, and heat it gently in a double boiler until the beeswax is melted and the mixture is smooth. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify, and your Humulus lupulus herbal cream is ready to use.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for anxiety

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Luteolin is good for anxiety because it has been shown to have a calming effect on the brain by reducing the production of stress chemicals and promoting the release of serotonin, a natural mood stabilizer.
Apigenin is good for anxiety because it has a calming effect on the brain and nervous system, helping to reduce feelings of stress and anxiety by interacting with neurotransmitters such as GABA.

Rosmarinic acid in HUMULUS LUPULUS has been shown to be effective in reducing anxiety due to its ability to interact with brain chemicals, specifically GABA receptors, to promote relaxation and calmness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a natural herb that has been used for centuries to help calm the mind and body. When applied topically in a cream form, its active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and beta-pinene, can be absorbed into the skin, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. Melissa officinalis cream has a soothing effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate feelings of stress and worry. By reducing muscle tension and promoting a good night's sleep, it can also help alleviate symptoms of anxiety. Furthermore, the calming properties of Melissa officinalis can help reduce symptoms of anxiety disorders, such as generalized anxiety disorder and post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD). Additionally, its natural and gentle formula makes it a suitable option for sensitive skin, and it can be used in combination with other relaxation techniques, such as deep breathing and meditation, to enhance its benefits.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Humulus lupulus herbal creams that help with anxiety, first you will need to gather the necessary ingredients. You'll need dried Humulus lupulus flowers, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few drops of a calming essential oil like lavender. Start by infusing the dried Humulus lupulus flowers in the carrier oil in a small saucepan over low heat for about 2 hours, allowing the flowers to release their properties into the oil. Next, strain the oil and discard the flowers, then melt the beeswax in a separate saucepan. Once the beeswax is melted, slowly add it to the infused oil, whisking continuously to avoid lumps forming. Finally, add a few drops of the calming essential oil and pour the mixture into a small tin or jar, allowing it to cool and solidify into a soothing cream that you can apply to your skin to help reduce anxiety.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for anxiety

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for anxiety because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce stress hormones and promote relaxation in the brain.

Flavonoids are good for anxiety because they have been shown to act as natural relaxants, reducing stress and anxiety by promoting the production of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and GABA, which help to calm the mind and body.
Kaempferol is good for anxiety because it has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system by reducing stress and inflammation, promoting relaxation and improving mood.
Tannins are good for anxiety because they have been shown to act as a natural sedative, helping to reduce feelings of stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata cream is made from the extract of the passionflower plant. This plant has been used for centuries to help calm the mind and body. The cream contains chemicals that help reduce anxiety by slowing down brain activity and promoting relaxation. It can also help with sleep problems, which are often linked to anxiety. When used on the skin, the cream absorbs into the body and starts to work quickly, often within 15-30 minutes. It's a natural remedy that's free from harsh chemicals and can be a safe alternative to prescription medications. Many people find that using Passiflora incarnata cream helps them feel calmer and more focused, which can be especially helpful during times of stress and anxiety.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Humulus lupulus herbal cream that helps with anxiety, you'll need some basic ingredients and equipment. Start by drying and preparing the hops (Humulus lupulus) flowers by placing them in a low-temperature oven (150-200°F) for 1-2 hours, or by air-drying them in a dark, dry place. Once dried, grind the hops into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, mix 1-2 tablespoons of the hop powder with 1 tablespoon of carrier oil (such as coconut or olive oil) to create a paste. Add a few drops of a carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil) and a small amount of beeswax (about 1 tablespoon) to the paste, and heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture has a smooth consistency. Remove the cream from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container. This homemade cream can be applied to the skin as needed to help alleviate anxiety symptoms, but always do a patch test before using it on a larger area.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal creams for anxiety

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Avenanthramides are good for anxiety because they have been shown to reduce inflammation and stress in the body by interacting with the brain's GABA receptors, which helps calm the nervous system.
Ferulic acid is good for anxiety because it has been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system by reducing stress and anxiety-related hormones in the body.

Saponins in HUMULUS LUPULUS are good for anxiety because they have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels by regulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as GABA and serotonin.

Flavonoids are good for anxiety because they have been shown to promote relaxation and reduce stress by binding to and inhibiting the activity of certain enzymes involved in the breakdown of neurotransmitters like GABA.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa cream is often used to help people feel less anxious. The main ingredient, Avena sativa, is an oat extract that has been used for centuries to calm the mind and body. It is believed to work by reducing stress and anxiety hormones in the body, such as cortisol. When applied topically to the skin, the cream may help to slow down the heart rate and promote relaxation. This can be especially helpful for people who have trouble sleeping due to anxiety. The cream may also help to reduce feelings of restlessness and irritability, making it easier to focus and think clearly. Some people also find that it helps to reduce physical symptoms of anxiety, such as muscle tension and headaches.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Humulus lupulus herbal creams that help with anxiety, start by preparing the herbal extract. First, dry a sufficient amount of Humulus lupulus flowers and leaves, then chop them finely. Next, combine the chopped herbs with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar. Leave the mixture in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the jar every day or two, to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids. Now, mix the infused oil with a cream base, such as beeswax and shea butter, in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, stirring constantly until the mixture is smooth and well combined. Remove the cream from the heat and let it cool before transferring it to a clean jar. This Humulus lupulus herbal cream can be applied topically to the skin, helping to calm the mind and reduce anxiety.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, is a natural herb used for treating anxiety. Its cream form helps to soothe and calm the mind and body. The herb contains a compound called hyperforin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically as a cream, hyperforin can help to reduce stress and anxiety by calming the nervous system. The cream can be applied directly to the skin, particularly on the wrists or temples, where it can be absorbed quickly. By reducing inflammation and calming the nervous system, Hypericum perforatum cream can help to alleviate anxiety symptoms and promote a sense of calm and well-being. It can also be used in combination with other stress-reducing techniques, such as deep breathing or meditation, to enhance its effects.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Humulus lupulus herbal creams that help with anxiety, start by gathering dried hops flowers and carrier oils like coconut or olive oil. Combine 2-3 tablespoons of dried hops flowers with 1 cup of carrier oil in a small saucepan, and heat the mixture gently over low heat for about 10-15 minutes, allowing the oil to extract the beneficial properties from the hops. Next, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Let the infused oil cool down, then mix it with a natural emulsifier like beeswax or shea butter to create a smooth and creamy texture. Finally, transfer the mixture to a small jar and let it cool completely before use. When applied topically to the skin, the Humulus lupulus herbal cream can help reduce anxiety by promoting relaxation and calming the mind and body.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale cream, also known as ginger cream, contains a natural ingredient called gingerol. This compound helps to calm the mind and body. Serotonin helps to relax the muscles and calm the nervous system. The cream can also help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can be caused by anxiety. Additionally, the scent of ginger in the cream can have a calming effect on the mind, helping to reduce feelings of anxiety and stress. remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams have been used to help people with anxiety. This is because the Ginkgo biloba plant is believed to improve blood flow to the brain. When we're anxious, our brain's blood flow can slow down, making it harder to think clearly and feel calm. By increasing blood flow, Ginkgo biloba creams may help our brain work better and reduce feelings of anxiety. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has antioxidants that can protect the brain from damage caused by stress and anxiety. Some people also think that Ginkgo biloba can help regulate mood and reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression. The active ingredients in Ginkgo biloba creams, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, may work together to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Humulus lupulus herbal creams that help with anxiety, start by gathering fresh or dried hops flowers, which contain the calming properties of the Humulus lupulus plant. Begin by making a hop tea by steeping a tablespoon of the flowers in a cup of boiling water for about 5-7 minutes, then straining and setting it aside to cool. Next, combine the cooled hop tea with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a small saucepan, in a ratio of about 1:10 (one part hop tea to ten parts carrier oil). Heat the mixture gently over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the oil has absorbed the herbal properties of the hop tea. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool before straining the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container. Finally, add a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter to the cooled mixture, stirring until it's fully incorporated, and then pour the cream into a clean jar. This homemade Humulus lupulus cream can be applied topically to the skin to help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation.

Combine the dried hops flowers with a carrier oil in a small saucepan and gently heat it over low heat for about 30 minutes to release the plant's active compounds. Then, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids. remedies.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus cream, also known as lupulin, is derived from the flowers of the hop plant. It's been used for centuries to help people relax and reduce anxiety. The active ingredients in the cream, including lupulins and flavonoids, have a calming effect on the body. They help slow down the heart rate and lower blood pressure, which can become elevated when we're feeling anxious. When applied to the skin, the cream can also help reduce feelings of stress and promote a good night's sleep. The calming properties of Humulus lupulus cream may also help reduce muscle tension and improve mood, making it easier to deal with daily challenges. Some people find that using the cream before bed helps them feel more relaxed and prepared for the next day.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Humulus lupulus herbal creams that help with anxiety, you'll need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, you'll need to prepare the herb by drying a few sprigs of Humulus lupulus, also known as hop flowers, in a warm and dry place or by using a dehydrator. Once dried, you can grind the flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, combine the powdered hop flowers with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a small saucepan, and heat it gently over low heat for about 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches a temperature of around 160-180°F. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids. Finally, add a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax to the liquid mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated, then pour the mixture into a small tin or jar and let it cool completely before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a helpful tool in managing anxiety. Two herbs commonly used in these creams are Lavandula angustifolia and Valeriana officinalis. Lavandula angustifolia, also known as lavender, contains compounds that can calm the mind and body. It has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality. Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is another herb used to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. It has been used for centuries to treat restlessness and insomnia.  When we experience anxiety, it can be overwhelming and affect many areas of our lives, including our relationships, work, and daily activities. Anxiety can also lead to other issues, such as sleep problems, digestive issues, and a weakened immune system. By using herbal creams that contain Lavandula angustifolia and Valeriana officinalis, we can begin to calm our minds and bodies, reducing our anxiety levels and improving our overall well-being. This can help us to feel more in control, confident, and able to handle life's challenges. By addressing anxiety and promoting relaxation, we can improve our sleep, relationships, and daily functioning, ultimately leading to a better quality of life.
supplementary_best: A combination of 'Lavandula angustifolia', 'Passiflora incarnata', and 'Melissa officinalis' can help remedy anxiety. Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender, has a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. Passiflora incarnata, or passionflower, has a sedative effect that can help calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and insomnia. Meanwhile, Melissa officinalis, or lemon balm, has a gentle sedative effect, reducing stress and anxiety while promoting a good night's sleep. This combination works well together because they target different aspects of anxiety: Lavandula angustifolia helps with relaxation, Passiflora incarnata helps with calming the nervous system, and Melissa officinalis helps with stress reduction. By combining these three herbs, you can address anxiety from multiple angles, creating a more effective remedy.
supplementary_avoid: If you have anxiety, there are some herbal creams you should avoid. One such herb is Kava (Piper methysticum), which can make anxiety worse in some people. Kava works by affecting the brain's chemical balance, but it can have the opposite effect in people who are already anxious.  Another herb to be cautious with is Skullcap (Scutellaria lateriflora), which can have a paradoxical effect on anxiety. While it's often used to calm the mind, it can make some people feel more anxious or restless. This is because Skullcap can interact with other medications, including those used to treat anxiety.  Some herbal creams also contain Ephedra (Ephedra sinica), which is a stimulant that can increase anxiety and heart rate. This herb can also raise blood pressure, making it a concern for people with anxiety who may already be experiencing cardiovascular issues.  Ginseng (Panax ginseng) is another herb to be careful with, especially in large quantities. It can increase energy and alertness, but it can also make anxiety worse in some individuals. This is because Ginseng can stimulate the brain and nervous system, making it difficult to relax and calm down.  Finally, some herbal creams contain Yohimbine (Pausinystalia johimbe), which is a stimulant that can increase anxiety and heart rate. This herb can also raise blood pressure and interact with other medications, making it a concern for people with anxiety.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aromatherapy with essential oils like lavender and chamomile can help with anxiety. These oils can be inhaled through steam inhalation, added to bath water, or used in a diffuser. When inhaled, they can affect the brain's limbic system, which controls emotions and mood. This can lead to a calming effect and reduce anxiety levels. Another natural remedy is spending time in nature, such as walking in a park or sitting by a river. Being in nature has been shown to lower cortisol levels, which are associated with stress and anxiety. This is thought to be due to the calming effects of natural sounds and visuals. Listening to soothing music or nature sounds, such as rain or ocean waves, can also help calm the mind and reduce anxiety. This is because our brains are wired to respond to rhythm and melody, and certain sounds can trigger a relaxation response. Exercise, such as yoga or walking, can also help reduce anxiety by releasing endorphins, which are natural mood-boosters.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have anxiety are often also affected by other health issues. One common problem is insomnia, which is difficulty sleeping. Anxiety can make it hard to relax and fall asleep. Medicinal herbs like chamomile and lavender can help calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep. Another issue is digestive problems, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Anxiety can cause stomach pain, nausea, and diarrhea. Herbs like peppermint and ginger can soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation. Anxiety can also lead to migraines and headaches. Herbs like feverfew and butterbur can help reduce pain and inflammation. Many people with anxiety also experience muscle tension, which can lead to chronic pain. Herbs like passionflower and valerian root can help relax the muscles and reduce pain. Additionally, anxiety can affect the immune system, making people more susceptible to illnesses like the common cold. Herbs like echinacea and ginseng can help boost the immune system and fight off infections.
